name: CI

on:

  pull_request:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:

display.text:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-x64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-vulkan
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-egl
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-simulator-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: windows-x64-vulkan
            runner: windows-2022
          - mise_env: windows-x64-wgl
            runner: windows-2022
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run test

      - name: Build Java FFM binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build
      - name: Build Java JNI binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-jni:build
      - name: Test .NET binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/dotnet:test
      # linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871
      # windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'linux-arm64-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build
      - name: Test Rust binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
      - name: Test Swift binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/swift:test
      - name: Test Zig binding
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/zig:ci

      - name: Build LWJGL example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan') }}
        run: mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build
      - name: Test Rust example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/rust-map:ci
      - name: Build Swift example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env == 'macos-arm64-metal' }}
        run: mise run //examples/swift-map:build
      - name: Build Zig example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-map:build
      - name: Run Zig readback example
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true

state.title: .github/workflows/ci.yml

name: Dev Container Image

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - .devcontainer/**
      - .mise/**
      - .miserc.toml
      - .github/workflows/devcontainer-image.yml
      - mise.lock
      - mise.toml
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - .devcontainer/**
      - .mise/**
      - .miserc.toml
      - .github/workflows/devcontainer-image.yml
      - mise.lock

display.text:
name: Dev Container Image

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - .devcontainer/**
      - .mise/**
      - .miserc.toml
      - .github/workflows/devcontainer-image.yml
      - mise.lock
      - mise.toml
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - .devcontainer/**
      - .mise/**
      - .miserc.toml
      - .github/workflows/devcontainer-image.yml
      - mise.lock
      - mise.toml
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: devcontainer-image-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

env:
  IMAGE: ghcr.io/maplibre/maplibre-native-ffi/devcontainer

jobs:
  build:
    name: build / ${{ matrix.arch }}
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' || github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
    permissions:
      contents: read
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 90
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - arch: amd64
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - arch: arm64
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - name: Install image tooling
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install --no-install-recommends --yes skopeo
      - name: Build image
        run: mise run //.devcontainer:build --tag "maplibre-native-ffi-devcontainer:ci-${{ matrix.arch }}"

  release:
    name: release / ${{ matrix.arch }}
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name != 'pull_request'
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 90
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - arch: amd64
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - arch: arm64
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - name: Install image tooling
        run: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install --no-install-recommends --yes skopeo
      - name: Log in to GHCR
        run: echo "${{ github.token }}" | docker login ghcr.io --username "${{ github.actor }}" --password-stdin
      - name: Build and push image
        run: mise run //.devcontainer:build --tag "$IMAGE:main-${{ matrix.arch }}" --push

  manifest:
    name: manifest
    permissions:
      packages: write
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name != 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: release
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - name: Log in to GHCR
        run: echo "${{ github.token }}" | docker login ghcr.io --username "${{ github.actor }}" --password-stdin
      - name: Publish multi-arch tag
        run: |
          docker buildx imagetools create \
            --tag "$IMAGE:main" \
            "$IMAGE:main-amd64" \
            "$IMAGE:main-arm64"

state.title: .github/workflows/devcontainer-image.yml

display.text:
name: Docs Pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: pages
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  build:
    name: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - uses: actions/configure-pages@45bfe0192ca1faeb007ade9deae92b16b8254a0d # v6.0.0
      - run: mise run //docs:build
      - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@fc324d3547104276b827a68afc52ff2a11cc49c9 # v5.0.0
        with:
          path: docs/dist

  deploy:
    name: deploy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    timeout-minutes: 10
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - id: deployment

name: Mirror to Codeberg

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ["**"]

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  mirror:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: codeberg
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:

display.text:
name: Mirror to Codeberg

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ["**"]

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  mirror:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: codeberg
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
        with:
          ref: main
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Push mirror
        env:
          CODEBERG_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.CODEBERG_USERNAME }}
          CODEBERG_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CODEBERG_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          {
            printf 'machine codeberg.org\nlogin %s\npassword %s\n' \
              "$CODEBERG_USERNAME" "$CODEBERG_TOKEN"
          } > ~/.netrc
          chmod 600 ~/.netrc

          git remote add mirror https://codeberg.org/maplibre/maplibre-native-ffi.git
          git fetch origin --tags
          git push mirror main
          git push mirror --tags

# Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs

experimental_monorepo_root = true

pin = true
experimental = true
python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"

windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'

# On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before

display.text:
# https://mise.jdx.dev
# Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs
# repository tasks.

experimental_monorepo_root = true

[settings]
lockfile = true
lockfile_platforms = ["linux-x64", "linux-arm64", "macos-arm64", "windows-x64"]
pin = true
experimental = true
python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"
windows_default_file_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; exec ""C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe"" ""$@""" _'
windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'
unix_default_file_shell_args = "bash"
unix_default_inline_shell_args = "bash -e -lc"
# On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before
# mise-managed Python. Keep mise tool paths first so file tasks run pinned tools.
activate_aggressive = true

[monorepo]
config_roots = [".devcontainer", "bindings/*", "examples/*", "docs"]

[tools]
# General tooling
"aqua:jdx/usage" = "3.3.0" # CLI completion for mise tasks
"aqua:jdx/hk" = "1.45.0" # Linter orchestration
"aqua:apple/pkl" = "0.31.0" # hk config dependency
"aqua:dprint/dprint" = "0.53.2" # Formatter orchestration
"github:sargunv/jvl" = "2026.05.03" # JSON validator
"aqua:rhysd/actionlint" = "1.7.8" # GH Actions linter
"aqua:koalaman/shellcheck" = "0.11.0" # Shell script linter (used by actionlint)

# C/C++ ecosystem
"github:prefix-dev/pixi" = "0.68.1"
"aqua:Kitware/CMake" = "4.3.3"
"aqua:ninja-build/ninja" = "1.13.2"
"conda:clang-format" = "22.1.7"
"conda:clang-tools" = "22.1.7"
"conda:doxygen" = "1.13.2"
"conda:glslang" = "16.3.0"
"conda:pkg-config" = "0.29.2"
"conda:vulkan-tools" = "1.4.341.0"
"github:cmakefmt/cmakefmt" = "1.3.0"
"aqua:XcodesOrg/xcodes" = { version = "1.6.2", os = ["macos"] }

# Node.js ecosystem
"core:node" = "24.11.0"
"aqua:pnpm/pnpm" = "10.33.2"

# Java/Kotlin ecosystem
"core:java" = "temurin-25.0.3+9.0.LTS"
"aqua:facebook/ktfmt" = "0.62"
"github:google/google-java-format" = "1.35.0"

# Python ecosystem
"core:python" = "3.14.4"
"aqua:astral-sh/uv" = "0.11.11"

# Swift ecosystem
"core:swift" = { version = "6.3.1", os = ["macos"] }
"github:nicklockwood/SwiftFormat" = "0.61.1"

# Other ecosystems
"core:rust" = { version = "1.95.0", profile = "minimal", components = "rustfmt,clippy" }
"core:zig" = "0.16.0"
"core:go" = "1.26.3"
"core:dotnet" = "10.0.203"

[env]
UV_PYTHON = { value = "{{ tools.python.path }}", tools = true }
MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT = "{{config_root}}"
MLN_FFI_VARIANT = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE = "{{env.MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE | default(value=\"RelWithDebInfo\")}}"
MLN_FFI_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/include"
MLN_FFI_VULKAN_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/third_party/maplibre-native/vendor/Vulkan-Headers/include"
MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/include"
MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"
MLN_FFI_ZIG_TARGET = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT = "{{env.MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT | default(value=\"30s\")}}"
CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default"
PKG_CONFIG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib/pkgconfig"
LIBCLANG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"

[hooks]
postinstall = [
  { task = "sync-submodules" },
  "hk install --mise --quiet",
  "pixi install --locked --quiet",
  # conda-forge ships libclang-13.dll, but clang-sys on Windows searches for libclang.dll.
  # https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys/issues/213
  # https://github.com/conda-forge/llvmdev-feedstock/issues/376
  '{% if os() == "windows" %}libclang_bin="$(dirname "$MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR")/bin"; cp "$libclang_bin/libclang-13.dll" "$libclang_bin/libclang.dll"{% endif %}',
  "uv sync --locked --quiet",
  "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --loglevel warn",
  "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
  "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest bindings/dotnet/dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
  # Astro type stubs for vp typeCheck on docs sources (.astro/ is gitignored).
  "pnpm --filter docs exec astro sync",
]

[tasks.configure]
depends = ["//:deps"]
run = [
  '''
  cmake_args=(
    -S .
    -B "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
    -G Ninja
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE="$MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE"
    -DMLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND="$MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND"
    -DMLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER="$MLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER"
    -DMLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT="$MLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT"
  )

  while IFS= read -r cmake_arg; do
    [[ -n "$cmake_arg" ]] && cmake_args+=("$cmake_arg")
  done <<< "${MLN_FFI_CMAKE_ARGS:-}"

  cmake "${cmake_args[@]}"
  ''',
  '''
  if [[ -f "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" ]]; then
    mkdir -p build/clangd
    compile_commands_link="build/clangd/compile_commands.json"
    compile_commands_tmp="${compile_commands_link}.tmp"
    rm -f "$compile_commands_tmp"
    ln -s "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" "$compile_commands_tmp"
    mv -f "$compile_commands_tmp" "$compile_commands_link"
  fi
  ''',
]

[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:configure"]
run = '''
build_args=(
  --build "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
  --parallel
)

cmake "${build_args[@]}"'''

[tasks.clean]
run = "rm -rf \"$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR\""

[tasks.deps]
description = "Install variant-specific managed dependencies."
run = '''
if [[ "$MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE" == "macos-arm64" ]]; then
  mise run deps:angle
fi
'''

[tasks.test]
depends = ["//:build"]
run = '''
(
  cd src/c_api/tests/zig
  zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
)
(
  cd bindings/zig
  zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
)
'''

[tasks.check]
run = "hk check --all"

[tasks.fix]
run = "hk fix --all"

[tasks.actions-up]
description = "Update and pin GitHub Actions steps."
run = "pnpm dlx actions-up"

[tasks._ktfmt]
description = "Called by dprint.jsonc"
hide = true
run = 'java -jar {{ tools["aqua:facebook/ktfmt"].path }}/ktfmt'
# ktfmt on windows doesn't work in git bash for some reason
shell = '{% if os() == "windows" %}cmd /c{% else %}sh -c -o errexit{% endif %}'

[tasks."ci:lint"]
run = [
  # Warm process-plugin cache before hk runs parallel dprint batches.
  "dprint output-resolved-config > /dev/null",
  "hk fix --all",
  "git update-index -q --refresh",
  # Lockfile drift is common across different mise builds
  "git diff --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
  "git diff --cached --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
]

[tasks."ci:vulkan-info"]
description = "Print the active Vulkan loader and driver summary."
run = "vulkaninfo --summary"

121:           "$env:SystemRoot",
122:           "$env:SystemRoot\System32\Wbem",
123:           "$env:SystemRoot\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0",
124:           "C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7",
125:           "C:\Program Files\Git\cmd"
126:         ) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) }
127: 
128:         "Path=$($paths -join ';')" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV
129: 
130:         $gradleTemp = $env:RUNNER_TEMP -replace '\\', '/'
131:         $gradleOptions = "-Djava.io.tmpdir=$gradleTemp"
132:         if ($env:GRADLE_OPTS) {

136: 
137:     - name: Install mise toolchain
138:       uses: jdx/mise-action@1648a7812b9aeae629881980618f079932869151 # v4.0.1

151:           echo "CMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
152:           echo "CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
153:         } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
154: 
155:     - name: Diagnose EGL setup
156:       if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
157:       shell: bash
158:       run: |
159:         mise exec -- bash -euo pipefail -c '
160:           env | sort | grep -E "^(EGL|LIBGL|MESA|LD_LIBRARY_PATH|__EGL|MLN_FFI|VK_)" || true
161:           command -v eglinfo

display.text:
name: Setup CI deps
description: Install sccache and mise toolchain

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - name: Enable Git long paths
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: git config --global core.longpaths true

    - name: Cache MapLibre Native source
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          third_party/maplibre-native
          .git/modules/third_party/maplibre-native
        key: mln-src-v2-${{ hashFiles('.gitmodules', 'cmake/mln_version.cmake') }}

    - name: Install sccache
      uses: mozilla-actions/sccache-action@9e7fa8a12102821edf02ca5dbea1acd0f89a2696 # v0.0.10

    - name: Use Linux software graphics drivers
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
      shell: bash
      run: |
        sudo apt-get update
        sudo apt-get install --no-install-recommends --yes \
          libegl-mesa0 \
          libgl1-mesa-dri \
          libvulkan1 \
          mesa-utils \
          mesa-utils-extra \
          mesa-vulkan-drivers
        {
          echo "EGL_PLATFORM=surfaceless"
          echo "LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=true"
        } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

    - name: Use Windows software graphics drivers
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $mesaVersion = "26.0.8"
        $mesaArchive = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "mesa3d.7z"
        $mesaRoot = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "mesa3d"
        $mesaUrl = "https://github.com/pal1000/mesa-dist-win/releases/download/$mesaVersion/mesa3d-$mesaVersion-release-msvc.7z"

        Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $mesaUrl -OutFile $mesaArchive
        $expectedHash = "A438C26C2752726916E455F9AD121F8A7E3CFECF8626251ABF8E5B3E129D8497"
        $actualHash = (Get-FileHash $mesaArchive -Algorithm SHA256).Hash
        if ($actualHash -ne $expectedHash) {
          throw "Mesa archive hash mismatch: expected $expectedHash, got $actualHash"
        }
        7z x $mesaArchive "-o$mesaRoot" -y | Out-Null

        $mesaWglDriver = Get-ChildItem $mesaRoot -Recurse -Filter "libgallium_wgl.dll" |
          Where-Object { $_.FullName -match "x64" } |
          Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $mesaWglDriver) {
          throw "Missing Mesa WGL driver in $mesaArchive"
        }

        $driverManifest = Get-ChildItem $mesaRoot -Recurse -Filter "lvp_icd.x86_64.json" |
          Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $driverManifest) {
          throw "Missing lavapipe Vulkan ICD manifest in $mesaArchive"
        }

        $mesaBin = $mesaWglDriver.Directory.FullName
        $mesaWglSystemDriver = Join-Path $env:WINDIR "System32\mesadrv.dll"
        Copy-Item $mesaWglDriver.FullName $mesaWglSystemDriver -Force

        $openglDriverKey = "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\OpenGLDrivers\MSOGL"
        New-Item -Path $openglDriverKey -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name DLL -PropertyType String -Value "mesadrv.dll" -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name DriverVersion -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name Flags -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name Version -PropertyType DWord -Value 2 -Force | Out-Null

        $driverKey = "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Khronos\Vulkan\Drivers"
        New-Item -Path $driverKey -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $driverKey -Name $driverManifest.FullName -PropertyType DWord -Value 0 -Force | Out-Null

        "GALLIUM_DRIVER=llvmpipe" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV
        "Path=$mesaBin;$env:Path" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

    - name: Cache Cargo packages
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          ~/.cargo/registry/index/
          ~/.cargo/registry/cache/
          ~/.cargo/git/db/
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-cargo-registry-v1-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}

    - name: Cache Gradle
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          ~/.gradle/caches
          ~/.gradle/wrapper
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-gradle-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle*', '**/gradle-wrapper.properties') }}
        restore-keys: |
          ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-gradle-

    - name: Trim Windows PATH before mise activation
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $workspaceDrive = Split-Path -Qualifier $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE
        # Git Bash maps /tmp to the checkout drive; create it before mise hooks run.
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Join-Path $workspaceDrive "tmp") | Out-Null

        $paths = @(
          if ($env:SCCACHE_PATH) { Split-Path -Parent $env:SCCACHE_PATH }
          if ($env:Path) { $env:Path -split ';' }
          "C:\Program Files\Git\bin",
          "C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32",
          "$env:SystemRoot",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32\Wbem",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0",
          "C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7",
          "C:\Program Files\Git\cmd"
        ) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) }

        "Path=$($paths -join ';')" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

        $gradleTemp = $env:RUNNER_TEMP -replace '\\', '/'
        $gradleOptions = "-Djava.io.tmpdir=$gradleTemp"
        if ($env:GRADLE_OPTS) {
          $gradleOptions = "$gradleOptions $env:GRADLE_OPTS"
        }
        "GRADLE_OPTS=$gradleOptions" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

    - name: Install mise toolchain
      uses: jdx/mise-action@1648a7812b9aeae629881980618f079932869151 # v4.0.1
      with:
        cache: true
        cache_key_prefix: mise-v2
        install: true

    - name: Wire sccache into CMake
      shell: bash
      run: |
        {
          echo "SCCACHE_GHA_ENABLED=true"
          echo "SCCACHE_GHA_VERSION=${MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE}"
          echo "SCCACHE_BASEDIRS=${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
          echo "CMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
          echo "CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
        } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

    - name: Diagnose EGL setup
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
      shell: bash
      run: |
        mise exec -- bash -euo pipefail -c '
          env | sort | grep -E "^(EGL|LIBGL|MESA|LD_LIBRARY_PATH|__EGL|MLN_FFI|VK_)" || true
          command -v eglinfo
          EGL_LOG_LEVEL=debug LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose LD_DEBUG=libs eglinfo || true
        '

    - name: Diagnose Vulkan setup
      if: ${{ contains(env.MISE_ENV, 'vulkan') }}
      shell: bash
      run: mise run ci:vulkan-info

    # The mise action may restore Rust metadata without all rustup-managed
    # component files. Repair the toolchain only when rustup cannot add the
    # configured components or the component binaries still cannot run.
    - name: Ensure Rust components
      shell: bash
      run: |
        ensure_rust_components() {
          mise exec -- rustup component add rustfmt clippy &&
            mise exec -- cargo fmt --version &&
            mise exec -- cargo clippy --version
        }

        if ! ensure_rust_components; then
          mise install --force rust
          ensure_rust_components
        fi

state.title: .github/actions/setup-ci-deps/action.yml

state.output:
<path>/app/AGENTS.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # AGENTS.md
2: 
3: This project is a C API for MapLibre Native, built for low-level language
4: bindings and host integrations that need a C boundary instead of direct C++
5: interop or the popular MapLibre Android/iOS SDKs.
6: 
7: ## Project map
8: 
9: - `include/` — Public C API headers (the stable ABI surface).
10: - `src/` — C++ implementation behind the C headers, plus render backend adapters
11:   (Vulkan, Metal, OpenGL) and the Zig test support shim.
12: - `bindings/` — Language bindings (Zig, Rust, Java FFM, Java JNI) that wrap the
13:   C API in idiomatic target-language interfaces.
14: - `examples/` — Small demo apps per language/backend (`zig-map`, `rust-map`,
15:   `zig-readback`, `lwjgl-map`, `swift-map`).
16: - `third_party/` — Vendored dependencies, primarily the MapLibre Native git
17:   submodule.
18: - `docs/` — Astro/Starlight documentation site and generated API reference.
19: 
20: ## Workflow
21: 
22: ```bash
23: # Install/refresh all tools, submodules, and dependencies
24: mise install
25: 
26: # List available tasks across the workspace
27: mise tasks --all
28: 
29: # Build the native library (also runs configure)
30: mise run build
31: 
32: # Build and run C API + Zig binding tests (also runs build)
33: mise run test
34: 
35: # Build and run Rust binding tests (also runs build)
36: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
37: 
38: # Headless smoke test — no display needed
39: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run
40: 
41: # GUI map app — use a brief timeout or run in background
42: mise run //examples/zig-map:run
43: 
44: # Build and test for a different variant (override auto-detected env)
45: mise -E linux-x64-egl run test
46: 
47: # Run formatters and linters on _all_ files (will stage affected files)
48: mise run fix
49: 
50: # Run formatters and linters on targeted files (will stage affected files)
51: hk fix [FILES...]
52: ```
53: 
54: Available mise envs: `linux-x64-vulkan`, `linux-x64-egl`, `linux-arm64-vulkan`,
55: `linux-arm64-egl`, `macos-arm64-metal`, `macos-arm64-vulkan`, `macos-arm64-egl`,
56: `windows-x64-vulkan`, `windows-x64-wgl`. The host-matching variant is selected
57: automatically via `.miserc.toml`.
58: 
59: Formatters and linters run automatically on pre-commit; you usually don't need
60: to run them manually.
61: 
62: The environment is managed by mise. If you need to run a command that's not
63: already a mise task, use `mise exec -- ...` so repository tools and dependency
64: paths are available.
65: 
66: ## Pull requests
67: 
68: When you open a pull request, follow the repository PR template and write
69: **Summary** and **Test plan** in at most one sentence each. The user will expand
70: the PR description if more detail is needed. More context:
71: [AI_POLICY.md](./AI_POLICY.md).
72: 
73: ## Project Invariants
74: 
75: ### General
76: 
77: - Campsite rules apply: leave anything you touch tidier than when you found it.
78: - The environment is defined by `mise`; pixi is the current desktop native
79:   library provider behind mise-managed paths.
80: - The bindings are meant to be low level and broadly analogous to each other and
81:   to the C API, exposing MapLibre concepts directly, while following language
82:   conventions for memory and thread safety. Prioritize safety, similarity, and
83:   idioms, in that order.
84: - Bindings expose MapLibre Native concepts directly. Add redundant APIs or
85:   convenience helpers only when they are strongly justified by target-language
86:   safety or ergonomics.
87: - Bindings do not reimplement native validation; they validate binding-owned API
88:   shape, state, lifetimes, and memory safety.
89: - We're currently in a prerelease state, so breaking API changes are allowed and
90:   encouraged over leaving backwards compatibility shims.
91: 
92: ### Prose
93: 
94: Use positive wording for guidance. Use negative wording for real prohibitions,
95: safety rules, and hard boundaries.
96: 
97: - Prefer: "Examples stay small and focused."
98: - Avoid: "Examples should not grow into full applications."
99: - Prefer: "Higher-level adapters may add execution models above this layer."
100: - Avoid: "This layer should not try to manage execution models for every
101:   possible host."
102: 
103: ### Specifications
104: 
105: For specification writing:
106: 
107: - Standalone and testable: each requirement should be checkable on its own,
108:   without pointing at an example or the current tree.
109: - Add, don’t restate or hedge: link to other docs instead of copying them; avoid
110:   “or equivalent”, unnamed MAYs, and vague outcomes.
111: - Scope by constraint: family-wide sections state behavior; platform- or
112:   API-specific rules belong in clearly labeled subsections.
113: 
114: ### Testing
115: 
116: - The bindings tests include broad integration coverage for the C/C++ layer on
117:   targets where they run.
118: - For tests that _must_ reach below the bindings, there are dedicated Zig tests
119:   in src/c_api/tests.
120: - Each binding's test suite should stand on its own for the C API domains and
121:   targets it supports, using public binding APIs to validate both native
122:   workflows and binding-owned safety behavior.
123: - Avoid trivial tests, tests that verify constants, tests that assert a negative
124:   (unless valuable), tests that simply test third party code; we want to keep
125:   our test suite robust and high-value.
126: - Example apps don't need tests.
127: - Every test skip should be strictly justified. We do not skip rendering tests
128:   because the CI environment doesn't support them; we fix the environment.
129: 
130: ## Project Docs
131: 
132: Read these docs before changing related code:
133: 
134: - [Binding Specification](docs/src/content/docs/development/binding-specification.md)
135:   for binding requirements and language binding changes.
136: - [Map Example Specification](docs/src/content/docs/development/map-example-specification.md)
137:   for example requirements.
138: - [Overview](docs/src/content/docs/development/overview.md) for project layout,
139:   workflow, and tooling.
140: - [Concepts](docs/src/content/docs/concepts.md) for project scope, ownership,
141:   threading, events, rendering targets, and host integration boundaries.
142: - [C API Conventions](docs/src/content/docs/development/c-conventions.md) before
143:   changing public C headers, C ABI behavior, callbacks, diagnostics, or render
144:   target contracts.
145: 
146: ## External Docs
147: 
148: Read these docs whenever relevant:
149: 
150: - `mise`:
151:   - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration.html>
152:   - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration/settings.html>
153:   - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration/environments.html>
154:   - <https://mise.jdx.dev/dev-tools/>

display.text:
# AGENTS.md

This project is a C API for MapLibre Native, built for low-level language
bindings and host integrations that need a C boundary instead of direct C++
interop or the popular MapLibre Android/iOS SDKs.

## Project map

- `include/` — Public C API headers (the stable ABI surface).
- `src/` — C++ implementation behind the C headers, plus render backend adapters
  (Vulkan, Metal, OpenGL) and the Zig test support shim.
- `bindings/` — Language bindings (Zig, Rust, Java FFM, Java JNI) that wrap the
  C API in idiomatic target-language interfaces.
- `examples/` — Small demo apps per language/backend (`zig-map`, `rust-map`,
  `zig-readback`, `lwjgl-map`, `swift-map`).
- `third_party/` — Vendored dependencies, primarily the MapLibre Native git
  submodule.
- `docs/` — Astro/Starlight documentation site and generated API reference.

## Workflow

```bash
# Install/refresh all tools, submodules, and dependencies
mise install

# List available tasks across the workspace
mise tasks --all

# Build the native library (also runs configure)
mise run build

# Build and run C API + Zig binding tests (also runs build)
mise run test

# Build and run Rust binding tests (also runs build)
mise run //bindings/rust:ci

# Headless smoke test — no display needed
mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

# GUI map app — use a brief timeout or run in background
mise run //examples/zig-map:run

# Build and test for a different variant (override auto-detected env)
mise -E linux-x64-egl run test

# Run formatters and linters on _all_ files (will stage affected files)
mise run fix

# Run formatters and linters on targeted files (will stage affected files)
hk fix [FILES...]
```

Available mise envs: `linux-x64-vulkan`, `linux-x64-egl`, `linux-arm64-vulkan`,
`linux-arm64-egl`, `macos-arm64-metal`, `macos-arm64-vulkan`, `macos-arm64-egl`,
`windows-x64-vulkan`, `windows-x64-wgl`. The host-matching variant is selected
automatically via `.miserc.toml`.

Formatters and linters run automatically on pre-commit; you usually don't need
to run them manually.

The environment is managed by mise. If you need to run a command that's not
already a mise task, use `mise exec -- ...` so repository tools and dependency
paths are available.

## Pull requests

When you open a pull request, follow the repository PR template and write
**Summary** and **Test plan** in at most one sentence each. The user will expand
the PR description if more detail is needed. More context:
[AI_POLICY.md](./AI_POLICY.md).

## Project Invariants

### General

- Campsite rules apply: leave anything you touch tidier than when you found it.
- The environment is defined by `mise`; pixi is the current desktop native
  library provider behind mise-managed paths.
- The bindings are meant to be low level and broadly analogous to each other and
  to the C API, exposing MapLibre concepts directly, while following language
  conventions for memory and thread safety. Prioritize safety, similarity, and
  idioms, in that order.
- Bindings expose MapLibre Native concepts directly. Add redundant APIs or
  convenience helpers only when they are strongly justified by target-language
  safety or ergonomics.
- Bindings do not reimplement native validation; they validate binding-owned API
  shape, state, lifetimes, and memory safety.
- We're currently in a prerelease state, so breaking API changes are allowed and
  encouraged over leaving backwards compatibility shims.

### Prose

Use positive wording for guidance. Use negative wording for real prohibitions,
safety rules, and hard boundaries.

- Prefer: "Examples stay small and focused."
- Avoid: "Examples should not grow into full applications."
- Prefer: "Higher-level adapters may add execution models above this layer."
- Avoid: "This layer should not try to manage execution models for every
  possible host."

### Specifications

For specification writing:

- Standalone and testable: each requirement should be checkable on its own,
  without pointing at an example or the current tree.
- Add, don’t restate or hedge: link to other docs instead of copying them; avoid
  “or equivalent”, unnamed MAYs, and vague outcomes.
- Scope by constraint: family-wide sections state behavior; platform- or
  API-specific rules belong in clearly labeled subsections.

### Testing

- The bindings tests include broad integration coverage for the C/C++ layer on
  targets where they run.
- For tests that _must_ reach below the bindings, there are dedicated Zig tests
  in src/c_api/tests.
- Each binding's test suite should stand on its own for the C API domains and
  targets it supports, using public binding APIs to validate both native
  workflows and binding-owned safety behavior.
- Avoid trivial tests, tests that verify constants, tests that assert a negative
  (unless valuable), tests that simply test third party code; we want to keep
  our test suite robust and high-value.
- Example apps don't need tests.
- Every test skip should be strictly justified. We do not skip rendering tests
  because the CI environment doesn't support them; we fix the environment.

## Project Docs

Read these docs before changing related code:

- [Binding Specification](docs/src/content/docs/development/binding-specification.md)
  for binding requirements and language binding changes.
- [Map Example Specification](docs/src/content/docs/development/map-example-specification.md)
  for example requirements.
- [Overview](docs/src/content/docs/development/overview.md) for project layout,
  workflow, and tooling.
- [Concepts](docs/src/content/docs/concepts.md) for project scope, ownership,
  threading, events, rendering targets, and host integration boundaries.
- [C API Conventions](docs/src/content/docs/development/c-conventions.md) before
  changing public C headers, C ABI behavior, callbacks, diagnostics, or render
  target contracts.

## External Docs

Read these docs whenever relevant:

- `mise`:
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration/settings.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration/environments.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/dev-tools/>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/environments/>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/tasks/>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/tasks/toml-tasks.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/tasks/file-tasks.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/tasks/task-arguments.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/tasks/task-configuration.html>
  - ... and many more pages. Browse the site if needed.
- `hk`:
  - <https://hk.jdx.dev/configuration.html>
  - <https://hk.jdx.dev/builtins.html>
  - <https://hk.jdx.dev/reference/examples/>
  - <https://hk.jdx.dev/pkl_introduction.html>
- `dprint`:
  - <https://dprint.dev/config/>
  - <https://dprint.dev/plugins/>
- `vp` / Vite+:
  - <https://viteplus.dev/guide/>
  - <https://viteplus.dev/guide/monorepo>
  - <https://viteplus.dev/guide/lint>
  - <https://viteplus.dev/guide/run>
- `pixi`:
  - <https://pixi.prefix.dev/latest/reference/pixi_manifest/>
  - <https://pixi.prefix.dev/latest/reference/pixi_configuration/>

part.text:
Now I understand the variant configs. Let me also check the tasks and remaining configs. Also, I need to think about what manifests to create.

The CI target support policy is the matrix of supported variants and what gets tested on each. I should create a declarative manifest that describes:
- Each variant (mise_env)
- The runner
- Which targets/jobs are enabled
- Any conditions

Then generate the workflow from this manifest. I also need a generator script and a CI check that the workflow is in sync.

Let me look at existing tasks and maybe how other generation is done.

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#MISE description="Download and verify the pinned ANGLE binary artifact for the active variant."
set -euo pipefail

manifest="$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/third_party/angle/manifest.json"

if [[ "$MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE" != "macos-arm64" ]]; then
  echo "ANGLE artifact is only configured for macos-arm64" >&2
  exit 1
fi

artifact="macos-arm64"

angle_meta="$(
  node -e '
    const fs = require("fs");
    const manifest = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(process.argv[1], "utf8"));
    const artifact = manifest.artifacts[process.argv[2]];
    if (!artifact) {
      throw new Error(`Missing ANGLE artifact ${process.argv[2]} in manifest`);
    }
    console.log(manifest.version);
    console.log(artifact.url);
    console.log(artifact.sha256);
  ' "$manifest" "$artifact"
)"

version="$(printf '%s\n' "$angle_meta" | sed -n '1p')"
url="$(printf '%s\n' "$angle_meta" | sed -n '2p')"
sha256="$(printf '%s\n' "$angle_meta" | sed -n '3p')"
angle_root="${MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}/third_party/angle/${version}/${artifact}"
archive="${MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}/third_party/angle/${version}/${artifact}.tar.gz"
marker="${angle_root}/.complete"
marker_value="${sha256} install-name-rpath-v1"

if [[ -f "$marker" ]] && [[ "$(cat "$marker")" == "$marker_value" ]]; then
  exit 0
fi

tmp_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$tmp_dir"' EXIT

mkdir -p "$(dirname "$archive")"
curl --fail --location --silent --show-error --output "$tmp_dir/angle.tar.gz" "$url"

actual_sha256="$(shasum -a 256 "$tmp_dir/angle.tar.gz" | awk '{print $1}')"
if [[ "$actual_sha256" != "$sha256" ]]; then
  echo "ANGLE archive SHA-256 mismatch for $artifact" >&2
  echo "expected: $sha256" >&2
  echo "actual:   $actual_sha256" >&2
  exit 1
fi

rm -rf "$angle_root"
mkdir -p "$angle_root"
tar -xzf "$tmp_dir/angle.tar.gz" -C "$angle_root" --strip-components=1
cp "$tmp_dir/angle.tar.gz" "$archive"

if [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
  install_name_tool -id @rpath/libEGL.dylib "$angle_root/libEGL.dylib"
  install_name_tool -id @rpath/libGLESv2.dylib "$angle_root/libGLESv2.dylib"
  codesign --force --sign - "$angle_root/libEGL.dylib" >/dev/null
  codesign --force --sign - "$angle_root/libGLESv2.dylib" >/dev/null
fi

printf '%s' "$marker_value" > "$marker"

part.text:
Let me explore the .mise config files more to understand all variants. I have read most. Let me think about design now.

Design proposal:
1. Create a manifest file `.github/ci-targets.json` (or TOML) that declaratively defines:
   - variants with their runners
   - groups of CI steps with conditions (e.g., native-build, native-test, java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, kotlin-native, rust, swift, zig, lwjgl, rust-example, swift-example, zig-example, zig-readback)
   - Each step has a name, a task, and an "enable" expression that can reference variant properties

2. Create a generator script (Python) at `scripts/generate-ci-targets.py` that:
   - Reads the manifest
   - Generates `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
   - Has a `--check` mode to verify the workflow is in sync

3. Add a mise task `ci:sync-targets` that runs the generator with `--check` (or regenerates)
4. Update the `hygiene` job to call `mise run ci:sync-targets --check` or similar to enforce sync
5. Test the generator

But wait — the user said "Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy." So I should reproduce the ci.yml as-is but from a manifest.

The tricky part is the conditions are complex:
- `startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
- `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
- `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'linux-arm64-')`
- `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-')`
- `contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan')`
- etc.

These can be expressed as policies in the manifest. I can define conditions like:
- `exclude: [ios-*]`
- `exclude: [macos-arm64-egl, ios-*]`
- `include: [macos-*]`
- `exclude: [macos-arm64-egl]`
- etc.

Alternatively, I can define a set of predicates like:
- `not_ios`
- `not_macos_egl_or_ios`
- `not_macos_egl_ios_windows_linux_arm64`
- `macos_not_egl`
- `vulkan`
- `not_ios`

I think a more elegant approach is to define per-variant capabilities or targets. Since the variants are already in `.mise/config.*.toml`, maybe we can add CI metadata there? But the user said "declarative" and "manifests" - probably a dedicated manifest is cleaner.

Let me think about the cleanest design. The manifest could be a JSON or TOML file. Given the project uses TOML heavily, a TOML manifest is natural.

Manifest structure idea:

```toml
[ci]
runners = { linux-x64 = "ubuntu-latest", linux-arm64 = "ubuntu-24.04-arm", macos-arm64 = "macos-latest", windows-x64 = "windows-2022" }

[[variant]]
name = "linux-arm64-egl"
target = "linux-arm64"
runner = "ubuntu-24.04-arm"
backend = "egl"

[[variant]]
name = "linux-arm64-vulkan"
target = "linux-arm64"
runner = "ubuntu-24.04-arm"
backend = "vulkan"
# ... etc

[[step]]
name = "Build native library"
task = "mise run build"
condition = "ios"

[[step]]
name = "Test native library"
task = "mise run test"
condition = "not ios"

[[step]]
name = "Build Java FFM binding"
task = "mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build"
condition = "not (macos-arm64-egl or ios)"

# ...
```

But conditions as strings need to be parsed. Maybe simpler to express as boolean properties on each variant, or to express conditions as arrays of include/exclude patterns.

Actually, the cleanest might be to define a list of variants and a list of "steps" where each step has:
- `name` - display name
- `run` - command
- `exclude` - list of variant name patterns (globs) to exclude
- `include` - list of variant name patterns to include (if provided, only those)

This mirrors the current if expressions.

Let me think about what each step's condition actually is:

From ci.yml:
1. Build native library: `startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
2. Test native library: `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
3. Build Java FFM binding: `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
4. Build Java JNI binding: same as 3
5. Test .NET binding: same as 3
6. Test Kotlin/Native binding: `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'linux-arm64-')`
7. Test Rust binding: same as 3
8. Test Swift binding: `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-')`
9. Test Zig binding: `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
10. Build LWJGL example: `contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan')`
11. Test Rust example: same as 3
12. Build Swift example: `matrix.mise_env == 'macos-arm64-metal'`
13. Build Zig example: same as 3
14. Run Zig readback example: `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`

So the conditions are variants of:
- ios only
- not ios
- not (macos-arm64-egl or ios)
- not (macos-arm64-egl or ios or windows-* or linux-arm64-*)
- macos not macos-arm64-egl
- contains vulkan
- macos-arm64-metal only

I could express these as property-based conditions. But to keep it simple and closely match current behavior, I'll use include/exclude glob patterns.

For each step:
- `include` = list of glob patterns; a variant matches if any pattern matches
- `exclude` = list of glob patterns; a variant is excluded if any pattern matches

If include is omitted, all variants are included by default; then exclude is applied.

Examples:
1. Build native library: include = ["ios-*"]
2. Test native library: exclude = ["ios-*"]
3. Build Java FFM: exclude = ["macos-arm64-egl", "ios-*"]
4. Kotlin/Native: exclude = ["macos-arm64-egl", "ios-*", "windows-*", "linux-arm64-*"]
5. Swift binding: include = ["macos-*"], exclude = ["macos-arm64-egl"]
6. LWJGL example: include = ["*vulkan*"]
7. Swift example: include = ["macos-arm64-metal"]

Wait, "contains vulkan" also matches macos-arm64-vulkan. But we also have windows-x64-vulkan. So include = ["*vulkan*"].

The generator would:
- Read the manifest
- For each variant, create a matrix include entry
- For each step, generate an `if` expression based on the include/exclude patterns

Actually, instead of generating the same complex if expressions, we can simplify by generating boolean checks per-step. But to preserve exact current behavior, we want the generated conditions to be semantically equivalent.

One challenge: the current conditions use things like `startsWith` and `contains`. To keep the generated output readable, I can generate expressions like:
- `startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
- `!startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
- `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')`
- etc.

But to do this from glob patterns, I need a pattern-to-expression translator. That could be complex.

Alternative: store the exact conditions as strings in the manifest, and just have the manifest be the source of truth. The generator validates that each variant has consistent policy and emits the workflow.

I think the better approach is:
- Manifest defines variants and steps
- Each step has a `condition` expression that uses `matrix.mise_env` directly, or uses a higher-level DSL

But the user wants it declarative. Let's make conditions declarative with include/exclude lists. For the generator, I'll implement a simple glob-to-expression translator that:
- For include-only patterns, generates `||` of matches
- For exclude patterns applied to all variants, generates `&&` of `!matches`

Hmm, but the exact current expressions are optimized (e.g., using startsWith for prefixes). If I generate from globs, the expressions might look different but be semantically equivalent.

Actually, a cleaner way: since we only have 11 variants, the generator can evaluate the condition for each variant at generation time and produce a list of included variants. Then the if expression can be generated as a disjunction of exact variant names or using prefix optimization. But that's overkill.

Simplest: generate `contains(fromJSON('["list","of","variants"]'), matrix.mise_env)` style conditions. But that's ugly.

Better: generate a boolean expression using `matrix.mise_env == '...'` disjunctions, and then optimize consecutive ones to startsWith where possible.

But this is getting complicated. Let me step back. The user wants:
1. Declarative manifests
2. Generated workflow
3. Enforce sync

The manifest doesn't have to use a high-level DSL if a simple one is clearer. We can have a manifest that is basically a structured representation of the current workflow, with each step's condition expressed as an expression over `matrix.mise_env`. This is still declarative because it's a manifest, not a workflow.

So the manifest could be:

```toml
[workflow]
name = "CI"
filename = "ci.yml"

[[job]]
id = "hygiene"
name = "hygiene"
runs_on = "ubuntu-latest"
timeout_minutes = 30
steps = [
  { run = "mise run ci:lint" }
]

[[job]]
id = "docs"
... 

[[job]]
id = "variants"
name = "variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}"
strategy = "matrix"
timeout_minutes = 120
env = { MISE_ENV = "${{ matrix.mise_env }}" }
matrix = { include = [
  { mise_env = "linux-arm64-egl", runner = "ubuntu-24.04-arm" },
  ...
]}

[[job.variant_step]]
name = "Build native library"
if = "startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')"
run = "mise run build"

...
```

This is very close to the workflow itself, just in TOML. Is this "declarative"? Yes. It's a manifest that describes CI policy.

But the user said "target support policy" — specifically about targets/variants. Maybe the manifest should focus on the variants and what they support, and the workflow structure is derived.

I think a middle ground is best:
- Define variants in a manifest with metadata (name, target, runner, backend, family, is_mobile, etc.)
- Define steps with a task and a predicate expressed in terms of variant metadata properties
- The generator evaluates predicates against variant metadata to produce the matrix if conditions

This is truly declarative. For example:

```toml
[[variant]]
name = "linux-arm64-egl"

Actually, before I write the manifest, let me reconsider whether to derive some capabilities from variant name patterns to reduce duplication. For example, `zig_readback = true` for all except ios. But explicit flags are clearer and more robust. The user wants declarative policy, so explicit is fine.

I'll write the manifest with all variants and steps.

One more thing: I want the generator to be able to format the output with dprint when available. To make the generator testable without dprint, I'll make dprint formatting optional: try `dprint fmt --stdin ci.yml` or similar, and if it fails, use raw output. But for the checked-in file, I want it formatted.

Actually, since I can't run dprint, I'll have the generator not depend on dprint. I'll generate clean YAML. I'll make the generator testable.

Let me write the manifest.

state.title: .github/ci-targets.toml

part.text:
Now let me write the generator script. I'll make it produce YAML carefully.

The generator will:
1. Parse the TOML manifest
2. Build the YAML output
3. Optionally format with dprint (if available)
4. Write or check

For the YAML generation, I'll write helper functions to format lists, mappings, etc.

Let me write the script.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate .github/workflows/ci.yml from .github/ci-targets.toml.

Run with no arguments to regenerate the workflow, or with --check to verify that
.the checked-in workflow is in sync with the manifest.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import difflib
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tomllib
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
MANIFEST_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".github" / "ci-targets.toml"
OUTPUT_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "ci.yml"

CHECKOUT_ACTION = "actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3"
SETUP_ACTION = "./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps"


def load_manifest(path: Path) -> dict:
    with path.open("rb") as f:
        return tomllib.load(f)


def format_yaml_value(value: object) -> str:
    if isinstance(value, bool):
        return "true" if value else "false"
    if isinstance(value, str):
        if value.startswith("${{") and value.endswith("}}"):
            return value
        return value
    raise TypeError(f"unsupported value type: {type(value)}")


def indent(text: str, spaces: int) -> str:
    prefix = " " * spaces
    return "\n".join(prefix + line if line else line for line in text.splitlines())


def generate_matrix(manifest: dict) -> str:
    variants = manifest["variant"]
    lines = ["    strategy:", "      fail-fast: false", "      matrix:", "        include:"]
    for name in sorted(variants.keys()):
        cfg = variants[name]
        lines.append("          - mise_env: {name}".format(name=name))
        lines.append("            runner: {runner}".format(runner=cfg["runner"]))
        for key in sorted(cfg.keys()):
            if key == "runner":
                continue
            value = format_yaml_value(cfg[key])
            lines.append("            {key}: {value}".format(key=key, value=value))
    return "\n".join(lines)


def generate_steps(steps: list[dict]) -> str:
    blocks = []
    for step in steps:
        name = step["name"]
        run = step["run"]
        lines = [f"      - name: {name}"]
        capability = step.get("capability")
        if capability:
            lines.append(f"        if: ${{% raw %}}{{{{ matrix.{capability} }}}}{{% endraw %}}")
        run_lines = run.splitlines()
        if len(run_lines) == 1:
            lines.append(f"        run: {run_lines[0]}")
        else:
            lines.append("        run: |")
            for line in run_lines:
                lines.append(f"          {line}")
        blocks.append("\n".join(lines))
    return "\n\n".join(blocks)


def generate_workflow(manifest: dict) -> str:
    steps = manifest["step"]
    matrix_block = generate_matrix(manifest)
    steps_block = generate_steps(steps)

    return f"""name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: {CHECKOUT_ACTION}
      - uses: {SETUP_ACTION}
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: {CHECKOUT_ACTION}
      - uses: {SETUP_ACTION}
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
{matrix_block}
    steps:
      - uses: {CHECKOUT_ACTION}
      - uses: {SETUP_ACTION}

{steps_block}

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true
"""


def format_with_dprint(content: str) -> str:
    """Format YAML content with dprint if it is available.

    Returns the original content if dprint cannot be found.
    """
    dprint = shutil.which("dprint")
    if dprint is None:
        return content

    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            [dprint, "fmt", "--stdin", "ci.yml"],
            input=content,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            check=True,
        )
        return result.stdout
    except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, FileNotFoundError):
        return content


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate the CI workflow from target manifests."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--check",
        action="store_true",
        help="Exit with an error if the checked-in workflow is out of sync.",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    manifest = load_manifest(MANIFEST_PATH)
    generated = generate_workflow(manifest)
    formatted = format_with_dprint(generated)

    if args.check:
        existing = OUTPUT_PATH.read_text()
        if existing != formatted:
            diff = difflib.unified_diff(
                existing.splitlines(),
                formatted.splitlines(),
                fromfile=str(OUTPUT_PATH),
                tofile=f"{OUTPUT_PATH} (generated)",
            )
            print(
                "Error: .github/workflows/ci.yml is out of sync with "
                ".github/ci-targets.toml.",
                file=sys.stderr,
            )
            print("Run `mise run ci:sync-targets` to regenerate it.", file=sys.stderr)
            print("\n".join(diff), file=sys.stderr)
            return 1
        print(".github/workflows/ci.yml is in sync with .github/ci-targets.toml.")
        return 0

    OUTPUT_PATH.write_text(formatted)
    print(f"Wrote {OUTPUT_PATH}")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

state.output: Wrote file successfully.

part.text:
I made a mistake in the `if` line generation. I used Jinja2 raw syntax `{% raw %}` which is wrong for a Python f-string. I need to escape the braces properly.

In the f-string, `{{` produces `{`, `}}` produces `}`. So to generate `${{ matrix.java }}`, I need:
`if: ${{ matrix.{capability} }}` but in an f-string, the `{{` would be literal `{`. Actually in an f-string, `{{` is a literal `{`. So:
`f"        if: ${{{{ matrix.{capability} }}}}"` would produce `        if: ${{ matrix.java }}`.

Wait, let me think. In an f-string:
- `{{` → `{`
- `}}` → `}`
- `{capability}` → substituted

So `f"if: ${{{{ matrix.{capability} }}}}"`:
- `if: $` (literal)
- `{{` → `{`
- `{{` → `{`
- ` matrix.` (literal)
- `{capability}` → substituted
- ` ` (literal)
- `}}` → `}`
- `}}` → `}`

Wait that's wrong. Let me count:
`f"if: ${{{{ matrix.{capability} }}}}"`
Characters: `i` `f` `:` ` ` `$` `{` `{` `{` `{` ` ` `m` ... `{` `c` `a` `p` `}` ` ` `}` `}` `}` `}`

Actually f-string processing: pairs of braces are reduced. `{capability}` is replaced. `{{` becomes `{`. So:
- `${{` → wait, `${{{{` is four braces. Pairs: `{{` + `{{` → `{` + `{` = `${{`
- ` matrix.`
- `{capability}` → `java`
- ` `
- `}}}}` → `}}` + `}}` → `}` + `}` = `}}`

So result: `if: ${{ matrix.java }}`. Correct!

So I should write:
`lines.append(f"        if: ${{{{ matrix.{capability} }}}})")`

But I wrote `f"        if: ${{% raw %}}{{{{ matrix.{capability} }}}}{{% endraw %}}"` which is nonsense in a Python f-string.

Let me fix that.

input.oldString:
if capability:
            lines.append(f"        if: ${{% raw %}}{{{{ matrix.{capability} }}}}{{% endraw %}}")

part.text:
Now, another issue: the f-string in `generate_workflow` contains `${{ ... }}` which will be interpreted as f-string braces. I used `{{ github.workflow }}` in the f-string. Let me check: in the f-string, `{{` becomes `{`, so `{{ github.workflow }}` becomes `{ github.workflow }`. Good. And `${{ github.event... }}` becomes `${{ github.event... }}`. Wait:
`$` + `{{` → `${`? No:
`${{` in f-string: `$` is literal, `{{` becomes `{`. So `${{` becomes `${{`. Yes correct.

Actually wait, in an f-string, `{{` is replaced by a single `{`. So `${{` becomes `${{` (dollar + literal `{`). Yes.

And `}}` becomes `}`. So `${{ github.workflow }}` in the f-string produces `${{ github.workflow }}`. Correct.

But wait, in my generate_workflow function, I have:
```python
    return f"""name: CI
...
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}
...
"""
```

The f-string will process these. `{{` → `{`, `}}` → `}`. So:
- `${{ github.workflow }}` → `${{ github.workflow }}` ✓
- `${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}` → `${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}` ✓
- `${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}` → `${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}` ✓

Good.

But in the required job step:
```python
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
```
This has single braces around `failure`, `cancelled`, `skipped`. Wait, these are inside `'` quotes, but f-string still processes braces. `'{failure}'` would try to evaluate `failure` as a variable. But I wrote `'failure'` (no braces). Good.

Actually, I need to be careful: in the f-string, `{needs.*.result` etc. is inside `${{ ... }}` so the outer `{{` and `}}` are literal braces. Inside, `needs.*.result` is literal. The single quotes are literal. Good.

But there's a subtle issue: f-strings also process `{` and `}` that are not in pairs. If I have a single `{` or `}` in the f-string, it might cause an error. In the generated YAML, do I have any single braces? Let's check the `toJSON` line:
```python
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
```
Here `{{` → `{`, `}}` → `}`. The single quotes are literal. So `echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'` → `echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'`. Good.

What about the `matrix.{capability}` line in `generate_steps`? That's not in an f-string, it's a regular string with f"...". Wait, it is an f-string: `f"        if: ${{{{ matrix.{capability} }}}}"`. The `{capability}` is replaced. `{{` and `}}` are literal braces. Good.

OK now let me test the generator.

part.text:
The f-string is stripping one brace. `${{` becomes `${` not `${{`. I was wrong about f-string behavior.

In Python f-strings:
- `{{` → `{`
- `}}` → `}`

So `${{` in an f-string becomes `${` (dollar + single `{`). Not `${{`.

To get `${{`, I need `{{{{`? Let's see:
- `{{{{` → `{{` (two pairs, each producing one `{`)

So `${{{{` → `${{`. Yes!

Similarly, `}}}}` → `}}`.

So I need to double the braces in the f-string. I.e., use `{{{{` and `}}}}` where I want `{{` and `}}` in the output.

Wait, but I already thought `{{` produces `{`. So to produce `{{`, I need `{{{{`. Yes.

Let me fix the generator to use quadruple braces in the f-string template.

Actually, the cleanest solution is to not use f-strings for the parts with GitHub expressions. I can use `.format()` or string concatenation. Or I can just escape properly.

Let me fix all the `{{` and `}}` in the f-string to be `{{{{` and `}}}}`.

Specifically:
- `${{ github.workflow }}` → `${{{{ github.workflow }}}}`
- `${{ github.event... }}` → `${{{{ github.event... }}}}`
- `${{ matrix.mise_env }}` → `${{{{ matrix.mise_env }}}}`
- `${{ matrix.runner }}` → `${{{{ matrix.runner }}}}`
- `${{ always() }}` → `${{{{ always() }}}}`
- `${{ contains(...) }}` → `${{{{ contains(...) }}}}`
- `${{ toJSON(needs) }}` → `${{{{ toJSON(needs) }}}}`

And in the `generate_steps` function, the f-string already uses `${{{{ matrix.{capability} }}}}` which is correct? Let me check:
`${{{{ matrix.{capability} }}}}`:
- `$` literal
- `{{{{` → `{{`
- ` matrix.` literal
- `{capability}` → replaced
- ` ` literal
- `}}}}` → `}}`

Result: `${{ matrix.java }}`. Good. That one is actually correct.

display.text:
def generate_workflow(manifest: dict) -> str:
    steps = manifest["step"]
    matrix_block = generate_matrix(manifest)
    steps_block = generate_steps(steps)

    return f"""name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: {CHECKOUT_ACTION}
      - uses: {SETUP_ACTION}
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: {CHECKOUT_ACTION}
      - uses: {SETUP_ACTION}
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
{matrix_block}
    steps:
      - uses: {CHECKOUT_ACTION}
      - uses: {SETUP_ACTION}

{steps_block}

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true
"""


def format_with_dprint(content: str) -> str:
    """Format YAML content with dprint if it is available.

    Returns the original content if dprint cannot be found.
    """
    dprint = shutil.which("dprint")
    if dprint is None:
        return content

    try:
        result = subprocess.run(
            [dprint, "fmt", "--stdin", "ci.yml"],
            input=content,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            check=True,
        )
        return result.stdout
    except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, FileNotFoundError):
        return content

return f"""name: CI

jobs:

    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: {CHECKOUT_ACTION}
      - uses: {SETUP_ACTION}
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: {CHECKOUT_ACTION}
      - uses: {SETUP_ACTION}
      - run: mise run //docs:build

    runs-on: ${{{{ matrix.runner }}}}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:

{matrix_block}

      - uses: {SETUP_ACTION}

{steps_block}

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{{{ always() }}}}
    needs:

      - docs

      - name: Fail if required jobs failed

        run: |
          echo '${{{{ toJSON(needs) }}}}'

      - run: true
"""

The original had: linux-arm64-egl, linux-arm64-vulkan, linux-x64-egl, linux-x64-vulkan, macos-arm64-metal, macos-arm64-vulkan, macos-arm64-egl, ios-arm64-metal, ios-simulator-arm64-metal, windows-x64-vulkan, windows-x64-wgl.

Wait, the original order is not alphabetical. Let me check:
Original order:

2. linux-arm64-vulkan
3. linux-x64-egl
4. linux-x64-vulkan

9. ios-simulator-arm64-metal
10. windows-x64-vulkan
11. windows-x64-wgl

My generator sorts alphabetically. That's a behavior change. I should preserve the original order to minimize diff and preserve behavior. Let me change the generator to preserve manifest order.

In Python, tomllib preserves the order of tables? TOML parsers are required to preserve order since TOML v1.0. Let me check: `tomllib` uses `dict` which preserves insertion order. So if I iterate over `manifest["variant"]` in order, I'll get the manifest order.

I'll remove the `sorted()` call in `generate_matrix`.

# Declarative CI target support policy.

# .github/workflows/ci.yml. After editing this file, run:

#
# The hygiene job enforces that the checked-in workflow stays in sync with this
# manifest.

[variant.linux-arm64-egl]
runner = "ubuntu-24.04-arm"
build_native = true
test_native = true
java = true
dotnet = true
kotlin_native = false
rust = true
swift = false
zig = true

part.text:
The diff shows:
1. Matrix entries now include capabilities (expected)
2. The "Configure native library" step now has a `name:` (I added it in the manifest) — this changes from `- run: mise run configure` to `- name: Configure native library\n  run: ...`
3. Blank lines between steps now (my generator adds blank lines)
4. Conditions changed from `matrix.mise_env` expressions to `matrix.capability`
5. Comments about Kotlin/Native removed (they were in the workflow, not in manifest)

I need to decide if these changes are acceptable.

The user said "Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy." The behavior is preserved: same variants run same steps. The YAML looks a bit different.

However, the blank lines between steps are not in the current workflow. dprint might remove them or keep them. Let me check if dprint would add blank lines. The current workflow doesn't have blank lines between steps. My generator adds them. I should remove the blank lines to match current style.

Also, adding a name to the configure step changes the display name. The original just showed the command. Is that a behavior change? In GitHub Actions, a step without name shows the run command as the name. So adding a name changes the displayed name in the UI. This is a minor change but not a behavior change in terms of what runs. However, to preserve current behavior exactly, I should not add a name to the configure step.

Wait, the manifest has `name = "Configure native library"`. The generator adds `name:` for every step. I can make the generator skip the `name:` if the step name is empty or omitted. Let me change the manifest to have an empty name for configure, or add a flag.

Actually, I can change the generator to not emit `name:` if the step name is empty. Then the configure step can have `name = ""` in the manifest. But TOML empty string is fine.

Alternatively, I can hardcode the configure step in the generator (no name) and only read binding/example steps from the manifest. But the manifest is supposed to be declarative for all steps.

Let me make the generator skip `name:` if the name is empty, and set configure step name to empty.

Wait, but all other steps have names. Let me set configure name to "".

Actually, I realize I can keep the name in the manifest but make the generator smarter: if the step name is exactly the run command, don't emit a name? No, that's weird.

Better: if `name` is empty string, don't emit `name:`.

Let me update the manifest to have `name = ""` for configure. And update `generate_steps` to skip name if empty.

Also, I should remove blank lines between steps to match current format. Let me update `generate_steps` to not add blank lines.

And I should add the Kotlin/Native comments back. These comments explain the policy. I can add them in the manifest or hardcode them in the generator. Since they're specific to a step, I can add a `comment` field to the step in the manifest.

Let me add `comment` support to the generator.

Actually, the comments are about why Kotlin/Native is excluded. They're valuable policy documentation. I'll add them to the manifest.

Let me update the generator and manifest.

part.text:
Now let me write a test. The test should:
1. Create a minimal manifest
2. Run the generator
3. Assert the output contains expected matrix entries and conditions
4. Test that --check passes when in sync and fails when out of sync

I can import functions from the generator script. But the script's `main` uses `MANIFEST_PATH` and `OUTPUT_PATH` constants. I'll need to refactor slightly to make it testable.

Let me refactor the generator to have a `generate` function that takes manifest data and returns the YAML. And `main` reads files and writes/checks.

Actually, I already have `generate_workflow(manifest)` which is testable. I can import it. For file-based tests, I can use temporary files.

Let me write the test.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Tests for scripts/generate-ci-targets.py."""

from __future__ import annotations

import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
GENERATOR = REPO_ROOT / "scripts" / "generate-ci-targets.py"


def run_generator(*args: str) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
    return subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, str(GENERATOR), *args],
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
    )


def test_check_passes_when_in_sync() -> None:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
        manifest = Path(tmp) / "ci-targets.toml"
        workflow = Path(tmp) / "ci.yml"
        manifest.write_text(
            """
[variant.linux-x64-vulkan]
runner = "ubuntu-latest"
build_native = true
test_native = true
zig = true

[[step]]
name = ""
run = "mise run configure"

[[step]]
name = "Test native library"
run = "mise run test"
capability = "test_native"
"""
        )
        result = subprocess.run(
            [sys.executable, str(GENERATOR), "--check"],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            cwd=tmp,
        )
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        generated = workflow.read_text()
        assert "mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan" in generated
        assert "runner: ubuntu-latest" in generated
        assert "test_native: true" in generated
        assert "if: ${{ matrix.test_native }}" in generated


def test_check_fails_when_out_of_sync() -> None:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
        manifest = Path(tmp) / "ci-targets.toml"
        workflow = Path(tmp) / "ci.yml"
        manifest.write_text(
            """
[variant.linux-x64-vulkan]
runner = "ubuntu-latest"
build_native = true
test_native = true

[[step]]
name = ""
run = "mise run configure"
"""
        )
        workflow.write_text("stale content\n")
        result = subprocess.run(
            [sys.executable, str(GENERATOR), "--check"],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            cwd=tmp,
        )
        assert result.returncode != 0
        assert "out of sync" in result.stderr


def test_generates_matrix_with_capabilities() -> None:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
        manifest = Path(tmp) / "ci-targets.toml"
        workflow = Path(tmp) / "ci.yml"
        manifest.write_text(
            """
[variant.linux-x64-vulkan]
runner = "ubuntu-latest"
build_native = true
test_native = true
java = true

[variant.ios-arm64-metal]
runner = "macos-latest"
build_native = true
test_native = false
java = false

[[step]]
name = ""
run = "mise run configure"

[[step]]
name = "Test native library"
run = "mise run test"
capability = "test_native"

[[step]]
name = "Build Java binding"
run = "mise run //bindings/java:build"
capability = "java"
"""
        )
        result = subprocess.run(
            [sys.executable, str(GENERATOR)],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            cwd=tmp,
        )
        assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
        generated = workflow.read_text()
        assert "mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan" in generated
        assert "mise_env: ios-arm64-metal" in generated
        assert "test_native: true" in generated
        assert "test_native: false" in generated
        assert "java: true" in generated
        assert "java: false" in generated


def test_preserves_manifest_variant_order() -> None:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
        manifest = Path(tmp) / "ci-targets.toml"
        workflow = Path(tmp) / "ci.yml"
        manifest.write_text(
            """
[variant.beta]
runner = "beta-runner"
build_native = true

[variant.alpha]
runner = "alpha-runner"
build_native = true

[[step]]
name = ""
run = "echo ok"
"""
        )
        subprocess.run(
            [sys.executable, str(GENERATOR)],
            check=True,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            cwd=tmp,
        )
        generated = workflow.read_text()
        beta_pos = generated.index("mise_env: beta")
        alpha_pos = generated.index("mise_env: alpha")
        assert beta_pos < alpha_pos


if __name__ == "__main__":
    test_check_passes_when_in_sync()
    test_check_fails_when_out_of_sync()
    test_generates_matrix_with_capabilities()
    test_preserves_manifest_variant_order()
    print("All generator tests passed.")

part.text:
Wait, my test imports functions but then uses subprocess with cwd=tmp. The generator uses `REPO_ROOT` constants, not cwd-relative paths. So running with cwd=tmp won't make it read the temp manifest. I need to either:
1. Refactor the generator to accept paths via arguments or env vars
2. Use monkeypatching in tests
3. Use a different test approach

I think the cleanest is to refactor the generator so that `main()` accepts optional manifest/output paths, defaulting to the constants. Then tests can pass `--manifest` and `--output` arguments.

Let me refactor the generator.

"""Generate .github/workflows/ci.yml from .github/ci-targets.toml.

Run with no arguments to regenerate the workflow, or with --check to verify that
.the checked-in workflow is in sync with the manifest.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import difflib
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys

from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
